questions.question: What was the main source of income for the British government in India?
questions.explanation: Land revenue collected from farmers was the main income for the British administration.

questions.question: What was the Permanent Settlement?

options.A: A system of collecting land revenue

questions.explanation: Permanent Settlement was a land revenue system introduced by the British.

questions.question: Who introduced the Permanent Settlement in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Lord Cornwallis introduced the Permanent Settlement in 1793.

questions.question: Who were zamindars?
questions.explanation: Zamindars collected land revenue from peasants for the British government.

questions.question: Who cultivated the land in villages?
questions.explanation: Peasants were farmers who cultivated land.

questions.question: What is indigo?

options.A: A plant used to make blue dye

questions.explanation: Indigo was used to produce blue dye for textiles.

questions.question: Why was indigo important to the British?

options.A: It produced blue dye for cloth

questions.explanation: Indigo dye was in high demand in European markets.

questions.question: Who forced peasants to grow indigo?
questions.explanation: European indigo planters forced peasants to grow indigo.

questions.question: What was the Indigo Revolt?

options.A: A protest by peasants against indigo planters

questions.explanation: Peasants revolted against forced indigo cultivation.

questions.question: When did the Indigo Revolt take place?
questions.explanation: The Indigo Revolt occurred in Bengal in 1859–1860.

questions.question: Why did peasants dislike growing indigo?

options.A: It reduced land for food crops

questions.explanation: Indigo cultivation reduced the land available for food crops.

questions.question: What did planters give peasants to make them grow indigo?
questions.explanation: Planters gave loans called advances to force peasants to grow indigo.

questions.question: What did peasants do during the Indigo Revolt?
questions.explanation: Peasants protested by refusing to grow indigo.

questions.question: Which crop was mainly affected by indigo farming?
questions.explanation: Indigo cultivation reduced the land available for food crops.

questions.question: What did the British government do after the Indigo Revolt?
questions.explanation: The Indigo Commission was appointed to investigate the revolt.

questions.question: Who helped peasants with loans in villages?
questions.explanation: Moneylenders provided loans but often charged high interest.

questions.question: Why did peasants borrow money from moneylenders?
questions.explanation: High taxes forced peasants to borrow money.

questions.question: Which region was famous for indigo cultivation?
questions.explanation: Indigo cultivation was mainly done in Bengal.

questions.question: What kind of crop is indigo?
questions.explanation: Indigo was grown mainly for trade and profit.

questions.question: Who controlled large plantations of indigo?
questions.explanation: European planters managed indigo plantations.

questions.question: Which activity shows peasant resistance?
questions.explanation: Refusing indigo cultivation was a form of resistance.

questions.question: What problem did peasants face due to high taxes?
questions.explanation: High taxes forced peasants into debt.

questions.question: What happened to many peasants because of loans?
questions.explanation: High interest loans trapped peasants in debt.

questions.question: Why did the British encourage indigo cultivation?
questions.explanation: Indigo was exported to Europe and was profitable.

questions.question: What does the Indigo Revolt show about peasants?
questions.explanation: The revolt showed peasants resisting colonial exploitation.
